title: Margyricarpus pinnatus
taxonomy: Margyricarpus pinnatus is classified in the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family includes a wide range of species, such as roses, apples, and pears, and is known for its showy flowers and edible fruits. Within the Rosaceae family, Margyricarpus pinnatus is a member of the subfamily Rosoideae, which includes plants with a rosaceous habit and five-petaled flowers. The genus Margyricarpus is a small group of plants that are native to the Andes mountains in South America, and is characterized by its small, white flowers and pinnate leaves. Margyricarpus pinnatus is a species within this genus that is known for its distinctive foliage and small, white flowers.
